exercises.title: Build LPS array for 'AABAAB'
exercises.instructions:
Manually compute LPS (failure function) for pattern='AABAAB'.
LPS[i] = length of longest proper prefix of pattern[0..i] that is also its suffix.
exercises.expected_solution:
Pattern: A A B A A B
Index:   0 1 2 3 4 5

LPS[0]: 'A' — no proper prefix → lps[0]=0
LPS[1]: 'AA' — prefix 'A' = suffix 'A' → lps[1]=1
LPS[2]: 'AAB' — prefix 'A'≠suffix 'B', prefix 'AA'≠suffix 'AB' → lps[2]=0
LPS[3]: 'AABA' — prefix 'A'=suffix 'A' → lps[3]=1
LPS[4]: 'AABAA' — prefix 'AA'=suffix 'AA' → lps[4]=2
LPS[5]: 'AABAAB' — prefix 'AAB'=suffix 'AAB' → lps[5]=3

LPS = [0, 1, 0, 1, 2, 3]

Meaning: on mismatch at position j, jump to lps[j-1]
  Mismatch at j=5: jump to lps[4]=2 (pattern already matched 'AA')
  Mismatch at j=2: jump to lps[1]=1 (pattern already matched 'A')

test_cases.expected:
Pattern: A A B A A B
Index:   0 1 2 3 4 5

LPS[0]: 'A' — no proper prefix → lps[0]=0
LPS[1]: 'AA' — prefix 'A' = suffix 'A' → lps[1]=1
LPS[2]: 'AAB' — prefix 'A'≠suffix 'B', prefix 'AA'≠suffix 'AB' → lps[2]=0
LPS[3]: 'AABA' — prefix 'A'=suffix 'A' → lps[3]=1
LPS[4]: 'AABAA' — prefix 'AA'=suffix 'AA' → lps[4]=2
LPS[5]: 'AABAAB' — prefix 'AAB'=suffix 'AAB' → lps[5]=3

LPS = [0, 1, 0, 1, 2, 3]

Meaning: on mismatch at position j, jump to lps[j-1]
  Mismatch at j=5: jump to lps[4]=2 (pattern already matched 'AA')
  Mismatch at j=2: jump to lps[1]=1 (pattern already matched 'A')

hints: Proper prefix: can't be the whole string
lps[0]=0 always (no proper prefix of length 1)
lps[j]: how many chars of pattern we can 'reuse' on mismatch

exercises.title: Trace KMP search
exercises.instructions:
Trace KMP search: text='AABAABAAB', pattern='AABAAB', lps=[0,1,0,1,2,3].
Show i, j at each step and matches found.
exercises.expected_solution:
text='AABAABAAB' (n=9), pattern='AABAAB' (m=6), lps=[0,1,0,1,2,3]

i=0,j=0: A==A match → i=1,j=1
i=1,j=1: A==A match → i=2,j=2
i=2,j=2: B==B match → i=3,j=3
i=3,j=3: A==A match → i=4,j=4
i=4,j=4: A==A match → i=5,j=5
i=5,j=5: B==B match → i=6,j=6
j==m=6: MATCH at index i-m=6-6=0! j=lps[5]=3
i=6,j=3: A==A match → i=7,j=4
i=7,j=4: A==A match → i=8,j=5
i=8,j=5: B==B match → i=9,j=6
j==m=6: MATCH at index i-m=9-6=3! j=lps[5]=3
i=9≥n: DONE

Matches at indices: [0, 3]
Note: text pointer i NEVER moved backward — only j jumped via LPS

test_cases.expected:
text='AABAABAAB' (n=9), pattern='AABAAB' (m=6), lps=[0,1,0,1,2,3]

i=0,j=0: A==A match → i=1,j=1
i=1,j=1: A==A match → i=2,j=2
i=2,j=2: B==B match → i=3,j=3
i=3,j=3: A==A match → i=4,j=4
i=4,j=4: A==A match → i=5,j=5
i=5,j=5: B==B match → i=6,j=6
j==m=6: MATCH at index i-m=6-6=0! j=lps[5]=3
i=6,j=3: A==A match → i=7,j=4
i=7,j=4: A==A match → i=8,j=5
i=8,j=5: B==B match → i=9,j=6
j==m=6: MATCH at index i-m=9-6=3! j=lps[5]=3
i=9≥n: DONE

Matches at indices: [0, 3]
Note: text pointer i NEVER moved backward — only j jumped via LPS

hints: i only moves forward. j jumps back via LPS
Match found: when j==m, record i-m, then j=lps[m-1]
LPS handles overlapping matches correctly

exercises.title: Build KMP LPS array
exercises.instructions:
Implement the LPS (failure function) array for KMP.
Test: buildLPS('AABAAB') → [0,1,0,1,2,3]
Test: buildLPS('ABCABC') → [0,0,0,1,2,3]
Test: buildLPS('AAAA') → [0,1,2,3]
hints: len: length of current longest proper prefix=suffix
Match: lps[i]=len+1, len++, i++
Mismatch: if len>0 → len=lps[len-1] (try shorter). If len==0 → lps[i]=0, i++

exercises.title: KMP Search — find first occurrence
exercises.instructions:
Implement full KMP search. Return index of first occurrence of pattern in text, or -1.

strStr('hello','ll') → 2
strStr('aaaaa','bba') → -1
strStr('mississippi','issip') → 4
hints: Same KMP logic: i forward, j via LPS on mismatch
Return i-m when j==m (complete match)
Return -1 if loop completes without match

exercises.title: Find ALL occurrences with KMP
exercises.instructions:
Find all starting indices of pattern in text (including overlapping).

findAll('AABAABAAB','AABAAB') → [0, 3]
findAll('AAAA','AA') → [0, 1, 2] (overlapping!)
findAll('abab','ab') → [0, 2]
hints: On match: record i-m, then j=lps[m-1] (not 0) — handles overlaps
AAAA with pattern AA: overlapping — j resets to lps[1]=1, not 0
Same loop continues after each match

exercises.title: Repeated Substring Pattern (LeetCode #459)
exercises.instructions:
Check if string s can be formed by repeating a substring.
'abab' → true ('ab' repeated)
'aba' → false
'abcabcabcabc' → true ('abc' repeated)

KMP trick: s+s contains s at position other than 0 and n iff s has repeated pattern.
hints: s+s contains s not at start/end → repeated pattern
LPS shortcut: lps[n-1] > 0 && n % (n-lps[n-1]) == 0
Unit length = n - lps[n-1]

exercises.title: strStr (LeetCode #28) — KMP vs Java built-in comparison
exercises.instructions:
Implement strStr using both approaches:
1. KMP: O(n+m) time
2. Java built-in: s.indexOf(t) — actually O(nm) worst case

Benchmark both on adversarial input: text='aaa...a' (100K 'a'), pattern='aaa...ab' (99K 'a' + 'b').
hints: Adversarial for naive: 'aaa...b' pattern in 'aaa...' text — backtracks n×m times
KMP stays O(n+m) on any input
Java's indexOf uses optimized heuristics but worst case is still O(nm)

exercises.title: Shortest Palindrome (LeetCode #214)
exercises.instructions:
Find shortest palindrome by adding chars to FRONT of string.

s='aacecaaa' → 'aaacecaaa'
s='abcd' → 'dcbabcd'

KMP trick: find longest palindromic prefix of s using LPS.
hints: LPS of s+'#'+rev(s) = length of longest palindromic prefix of s
'#' separator: prevents LPS from extending across s and rev(s)
Add rev[0..n-palindromeLen-1] to front: shortest addition

exercises.title: Rabin-Karp: Rolling Hash Pattern Search
exercises.instructions:
Implement Rabin-Karp string search using rolling hash.
Average O(n+m), worst O(nm).

Use: hash = hash * BASE + char_value (rolling: subtract first char, shift, add new char).

Find first occurrence of pattern in text.
hints: Rolling: remove leftmost char contribution, shift, add new rightmost char
power = BASE^(m-1): for removing the first char's contribution
Always verify on hash match (equals()) to handle false positives

exercises.title: LPS array for tricky patterns
exercises.instructions:
Compute LPS array for each pattern:
A: 'AAAAAA'
B: 'ABCDEF'
C: 'ABAB'
D: 'ABACABAB'
exercises.expected_solution:
A: 'AAAAAA' — all same chars
LPS = [0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
Every prefix is also a suffix. lps[i]=i for i>0.
On mismatch at j=5: jump to 4, then 3, etc. — many jumps but i stays put.

B: 'ABCDEF' — all different chars
LPS = [0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0]
No prefix equals any suffix. On any mismatch: j resets to 0.
KMP behaves like naive search for this pattern (no speedup).

C: 'ABAB'
LPS = [0, 0, 1, 2]
prefix 'AB' = suffix 'AB' at index 3 → lps[3]=2
Meaning: on mismatch after 'ABAB', we already matched 'AB'

D: 'ABACABAB'
LPS = [0, 0, 1, 0, 1, 2, 3, 2]
Breaking down: A=0, AB=0, ABA=1(A), ABAC=0, ABACA=1(A), ABACAB=2(AB), ABACABA=3(ABA), ABACABAB=2(AB)
Useful for text containing 'ABACABA...' patterns

test_cases.expected:
A: 'AAAAAA' — all same chars
LPS = [0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
Every prefix is also a suffix. lps[i]=i for i>0.
On mismatch at j=5: jump to 4, then 3, etc. — many jumps but i stays put.

B: 'ABCDEF' — all different chars
LPS = [0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0]
No prefix equals any suffix. On any mismatch: j resets to 0.
KMP behaves like naive search for this pattern (no speedup).

C: 'ABAB'
LPS = [0, 0, 1, 2]
prefix 'AB' = suffix 'AB' at index 3 → lps[3]=2
Meaning: on mismatch after 'ABAB', we already matched 'AB'

D: 'ABACABAB'
LPS = [0, 0, 1, 0, 1, 2, 3, 2]
Breaking down: A=0, AB=0, ABA=1(A), ABAC=0, ABACA=1(A), ABACAB=2(AB), ABACABA=3(ABA), ABACABAB=2(AB)
Useful for text containing 'ABACABA...' patterns

hints: All-same: lps[i]=i (each prefix equals each suffix)
All-different: lps[i]=0 (no prefix=suffix)
'ABAB': lps[3]=2 ('AB' prefix = 'AB' suffix)

exercises.title: Check if string rotation of another (KMP application)
exercises.instructions:
Check if s2 is a rotation of s1.
's1=abcde, s2=cdeab' → true (rotated by 3)
's1=abc, s2=def' → false

KMP trick: find s2 in s1+s1.
hints: s2 is rotation of s1 ↔ s2 appears in s1+s1
s1+s1 contains all rotations of s1
Use KMP for O(n) search

exercises.title: Count pattern occurrences using KMP — Zerodha log search
exercises.instructions:
Count how many times pattern appears in text (non-overlapping).

contains('AAAAAA','AA') → 3 (non-overlapping: positions 0,2,4)
contains('ABABABAB','ABAB') → 2 (non-overlapping: 0,4)
contains('hello world hello','hello') → 2
hints: Non-overlapping: reset j=0 after match (skip full pattern length)
Overlapping: j=lps[m-1] after match (partially reuse)
Same KMP framework, different post-match behavior

exercises.title: KMP Full Implementation with all LPS edge cases
exercises.instructions:
Implement production-quality KMP that handles:
1. Empty pattern → return 0
2. Pattern longer than text → return -1
3. Single-char pattern
4. Pattern = text → return 0
5. Unicode (treat as char array)

Benchmark vs String.indexOf() on 1M char text.
hints: Edge: empty pattern → always found at 0
Edge: m>n → impossible match
Always verify edge cases before benchmark

exercises.title: String matching in 2D matrix (KMP application)
exercises.instructions:
Find if word exists in 2D grid, moving right or down only.

grid=[['A','B','C'],['D','E','F'],['G','H','I']], word='AEI' → true (diagonal)
word='ADG' → true (down column 0)
word='CEF' → false

For right/down only: check each row (KMP) and column (KMP).
For diagonal: extract diagonals and search.
hints: Build each row/column as string, then KMP search
Extract rows/columns as strings first
O(n×m) total where n×m = grid size

exercises.title: Complete M31 T2 Pattern Matching Synthesis
exercises.instructions:
Build complete pattern matching toolkit:
1. buildLPS(pattern): O(m)
2. kmpFindAll(text, pattern): all occurrences including overlapping
3. isRotation(s1, s2): using KMP on doubled string
4. repeatedSubstringKMP(s): LPS end value check
5. All 4 tests pass
hints: kmpFindAll: j=lps[m-1] after match for overlapping
isRotation: find s2 in s1+s1
repeatedSubstring: lps[n-1]>0 && n%(n-lps[n-1])==0
